chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q1_analysis: The paper mentions verifying safety and liveness in a model checker (Murphi) and provides a GitHub link to the Murphi model at https://github.com/adarshpatil/apta. However, it does not provide links to working code for the full system implementation, Docker containers, or the simulation scripts used for the performance evaluation.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q2_analysis: The evaluation relies entirely on a simulator-based methodology (modified gem5) using traces generated from Python applications. There is no mention of physical hardware deployment, real testbeds, or massive-scale cloud deployments; it uses a 'toy' scale setup of 3 compute servers and 1 memory server.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q3_analysis: The paper has a clear, explicit section (Section III-B) detailing the fault model. It explicitly defines the threat as independent compute server failures/unavailability in a failure-prone datacenter environment and specifies that the DM server is assumed highly-available via replication.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q4_analysis: The paper provides a rigorous, data-driven analysis of performance trade-offs. It quantifies execution time speedups (21-90%), breaks down latency reductions for get/put operations, analyzes standard deviation for tail latency resilience, and includes sensitivity studies on bandwidth, latency, and compute capability.

major_weakness: The evaluation lacks experimental rigor as it relies entirely on a small-scale simulation (gem5) without physical hardware deployment or open-source code for the full system implementation.
